Aether Crystal
Race
Aetherial
Type
Champion
Faction
Aetherials
Aether Crystals are
Aetherial
creatures.
They drop an
Aether Crystal
item when destroyed.
Abilities
Summon Zombie
Summons 3
Walking Dead
8 Second Cooldown
6 Summon Limit
Aether Tendril
Aether Damage
3% reduction to Current Life
Aether Field (buff)
(4.5 to 10.5) Meter Radius
Aether Damage
5-R0% reduction to Current Life
Reflects 8% Damage

Aether Crystal Cluster
Race
Aetherial
Type
Champion
Faction
Aetherials
Aether Crystals Clusters are
Aetherial
creatures.
They possess the same skills as
Aether Crystals
but have additional resistances and spawn 3 levels higher than the player (thus using skills at a higher level).
Abilities
Summon Zombie
Summons 3
Walking Dead
8 Second Cooldown
6 Summon Limit
Aether Tendril
Aether Damage
3% reduction to Current Life
Aether Field (buff)
(4.5 to 10.5) Meter Radius
Aether Damage
5-10% reduction to Current Life
Reflects 8% Damage
Resistances
Pierce
50
Bleeding
100
Fire
15
Lightning
30
Aether
60
Poison
50
Vitality
30
Freeze
300
Knockdown
500
Petrify
300
Fear
500
Convert
500
Confusion
500

500

500

500

500

Aether Crystal Formation
Race
Aetherial
Type
Champion
Faction
Aetherials
Aether Crystals Formations are
Aetherial
creatures.
A larger version of the
Aether Crystals
, they possess the same skills but have additional resistances, and spawn 5 levels higher than the player (thus using skills at a higher level).
Abilities
Summon Aetherfire Zombie
Summons 1 Plague Walker
8 Second Cooldown
5 Summon Limit
Aether Tendril
Aether Damage
3% reduction to Current Life
Aether Field (buff)
(4.5 to 10.5) Meter Radius
Aether Damage
5-10% reduction to Current Life
Reflects 8% Damage

Aetherial Abomination
Race
Aetherial
Type
Champion
Faction
Aetherials
Aetherial Abominations are
Aetherial
creatures. They will spawn from certain Aether Crystal Clusters when destroyed.
Abilities
Passive
Physical Damage
Aether Damage
+15% Damage against Eldritch creatures
Charge - melee
130% Movement Speed
Physical Damage
Aether Damage
Stun for (0.5 to 1.9) Seconds
Aether Shards
offensive buff
2 Meter Radius
Aether Damage
Aether Shard Retaliation
1.2 Second Cooldown
Aether Damage
Aether Waves
20 meter Wave Distance
Physical Damage
Aether Damage
-20% Total Speed for 4 Seconds
Summon Aetherfire Zombie
Summons 8 Plague Walkers
8 Summon Limit
Resistances
Level 2
Hero/Boss Resistances
Physical: 20%
Pierce: 25%
Aether: 25%
Stun: 500%
Knockdown: 500%

Aetherial Behemoth
Race
Aetherial
Type
Champion
Faction
Aetherial Vanguard
Aetherial Behemoths
are a type of
Aetherial
creature, found throughout
Act 6
.

Aetherial Bloater
Race
Aetherial
Type
Common
Faction
Aetherial Vanguard
Aetherial Bloaters
are a type of
Aetherial
creature, found throughout
Act 6
.
Abilities
Skill Name
Skill Type
Damage Type
Passive
Physical Damage
7.5% Chance of +30% Physical Damage
Poison Damage over 5 Seconds
15% Chance of +10% Poison Damage with +33% Increased Duration
10% Chance to Avoid Projectiles
Poison Retaliation over 5 Seconds
Bite Weak
Melee
2 Charge Levels: 100%
2.5 Second Charge Level Duration
Physical Damage
Acid Damage
12% Reduction to Enemy's Health
Poison Damage over 8 Seconds
Generic Charge
(low speed)
Charge
5 Charge Levels: +15%, +30%, +45%, +60%, +75% to All Damage
100% Weapon Damage
+30% Movement Speed
+50% Attack Speed
+50% Casting Speed

Aetherial Crawler
Race
Aetherial
Type
Common
Faction
Aetherial Vanguard
Aetherial Crawlers
are a type of
Aetherial
creature, found throughout
Act 6
. They are summoned by Aetherial
Fleshweavers
.
Abilities
Passive
Physical Damage
8% Chance of +40% Physical Damage
Gory Eruption (Cast on Death)
2.5 Meter Target Area
Physical Damage
Aether Damage
6-12% Reduction to Enemy's Health
Resistances
Fire: 30%
Vitality: 30%
Masteries

Aetherial Gutburrower
Race
Aetherial
Type
Common
Faction
Aetherial Vanguard
Aetherial Gutburrowers
are a type of
Aetherial
creature, found throughout
Act 6
. They spawn from slain
Aetherial Regurgitators
.
Abilities
Passive
Physical Damage
Aether Damage
8% Chance of +40% Physical Damage
10% Pierce Resistance
Poison Retaliation
Aether Spit
1 Projectile
2.5 Meter Radius
Aether Damage
Resistances
Physical
25%
Pierce
35%
Aether
25%
Sleep
500%
Stun
500%
Knockdown
500%
Trap
500%
Freeze
500%
Petrify
500%

Aetherial Imp
Race
Aetherial
Type
Champion
Faction
Aetherial Vanguard
Aetherial Imps
are a type of
Aetherial
creature, found throughout
Act 6
.
Abilities
Passive
Physical Damage
7.5% Chance of +30% Physical Damage
Vitality Damage
Aether Damage
10% Chance to Avoid Projectiles
Aetherfire Strike (melee)
Physical Damage
Aether Damage
30% chance to cast Aetherfire Projectile:
4 Second Duration
3 Meter Radius
Lightning Damage
Aether Damage
Aether Eruption (Cast on Death)
2.5 Meter Target Area
Physical Damage
Aether Damage
6% Reduction to Enemy's Health
Reduced target's Offensive Ability for 3 Seconds
Resistances
Fire: 30%
Vitality: 30%
Masteries

Aetherial Obelisk
Race
Aetherial
Type
Champion
Faction
Aetherials
Aetherial Obelisks are
Aetherial
creatures.
The  Obelisk has 2 forms. in the first it is anchored to the ground, in the 2nd (at 50% Health) it breaks free and adds a spinning Ground Slam ability.
Abilities
Zap
Aether Damage
Aether Conflagration
3 Meter radius
3 Second Duration
Aether Damage
Summon Burning Dead
5 Second Cooldown
Summons 4
Burning Dead
6 Summon Limit
Summon Fleshwarped Thrasher
8 Second Cooldown
Summons 1
Fleshwarped Thrasher
2 Summon Limit
Summon Fleshwarped Thrasher 2
8 Second Cooldown
Summons 2 Fleshwarped Thrashers
8 Summon Limit
Aether Slam (2nd form only)
7 Meter radius
Aether damage

Aetherial Regurgitator
Race
Aetherial
Type
Champion
Faction
Aetherial Vanguard
Aetherial Regurgitators
are a type of
Aetherial
creature, found throughout
Act 6
. On death they will spawn several
Aetherial Gutburrowers
.

Aetherial Scamp
Race
Aetherial
Type
Common
Faction
Aetherial Vanguard
Aetherial Scamps
are a type of
Aetherial
creature, found throughout
Act 6
.
Abilities
Passive
Physical Damage
8% Chance of +40% Physical Damage
Gory Eruption (Cast on Death)
2.5 Meter Target Area
Physical Damage
Aether Damage
6-12% Reduction to Enemy's Health
Resistances
Fire: 30%
Vitality: 30%
Masteries

======================================================================
PAGE: Aetherial Sentinel

Aetherial Sentinel
Race
Aetherial
Type
Champion
Faction
Aetherial Vanguard
Aetherial Sentinels
are a type of
Aetherial
creature, found throughout
Act 6
. On death they spawn a 2nd-stage
Aetherial Terraformer
.
Abilities
Skill Name
Skill Type
Damage Type
Passive
Physical Damage

Act 6
.

======================================================================

Aetherial Terraformer
Race
Aetherial
Type
Champion
Faction
Aetherials
Aetherial Terraformers are
Aetherial
creatures. They are a more powerful form of the
Aetherial Obelisk
.
The  Terraformer has 2 forms. in the first it is anchored to the ground, in the 2nd (at 50% Health) it breaks free and gains additional skills:
Claw Spin
and
Aether Slam
.
Abilities
Zap
Aether Damage
Vindictive Aether
25% Chance on Hit
5 Second Cooldown
5 Meter Radius
Aether Damage
+25% Damage to Eldritch Creatures
Despair Aura
16 Meter Radius
Aether Damage
-20% Total Speed
Aether Conflagration
3 Meter radius
3 Second Duration
Aether Damage
Summon Burning Dead
5 Second Cooldown
Summons 4
Burning Dead
6 Summon Limit
Summon Fleshwarped Thrasher
8 Second Cooldown
Summons 1
Fleshwarped Thrasher
2 Summon Limit
Summon Fleshwarped Thrasher 2
8 Second Cooldown
Summons 2 Fleshwarped Thrashers
8 Summon Limit
Aether Slam (2nd form only)
7 Meter radius
Aether damage
Claw Spin (2nd form only)
5 Meter radius
Physical Damage
Aether Damage

Level 2

Aetherial Titan
Race
Aetherial
Type
Champion
Faction
Aetherial Vanguard
Aetherial Titans
are a type of
Aetherial
creature, found throughout
Act 6
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Aetherial Vanguard
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Aetherial_Vanguard
======================================================================

Among the Aetherials that crumbled humanity's empire, in a devastating event now called the Grim Dawn, there were those who reached out beyond the veil first, the ones that would become the heralds of mankind's destruction.
They were the first to infiltrate the ranks of Erulan, the first to manipulate the unwitting humans to undermine their own cities and governments. This Aetherial Vanguard now spreads its corrupting influence from the ruins that once stood as proud beacons of the fallen empire.
But all this carnage was merely the beginning of their machinations. At the hands of the Aetherial fleshshapers, vile new creations stir within the depths of Malmouth. Whatever comes next may well mark the end of humanity's resistance.
Faction Rewards

======================================================================
PAGE: Aetherials
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Aetherials
======================================================================

Foolishly and greedily did the arcanists of Cairn delve into the magical veil known as the Aether, never once suspecting that something was looking back.
The Aetherials descended upon Cairn in what is now known as the Grim Dawn, possessing countless humans and butchering the rest. Most despicably of all, they began raising the fallen as mutated undead creatures, mindless foot soldiers for their invasion. Their very presence on Cairn is enough to mutate the wildlife, turning once docile creatures into vicious monstrosities.
But most strange of all is their apparent hatred for Chthonians, as they will battle against the horrors from the Void at every opportunity.
Aetherials
are one of two otherworldly
factions
present in the
Grim Dawn
universe. Killing
Aetherial
and
Aether Corruption
enemies will increase the players' negative Faction Reputation.
Quotes
"What is an Aetherial really?" - Player
"We were the servants of the primordial gods, but when our makers grew jealous of one another, we became soldiers in a cataclysmic war. Countless creations were eradicated by the ensuing conflict, countless millennia of our work erased in a flicker of godly anger. But none of that compared to the day one side chose to harness the darkness of the void in a desperate bid for victory. The results were catastrophic and our masters were cut down or scattered to the far reaches of creation. And we, the most loyal servants, were banished to the Aether for the simple crime of serving without question. There, stripped of our physical forms, we were forced to wander the emptiness of the stars for all eternity, the scars inflicted upon us by the void's minions still burning deep. That is until we encountered humans..." -
Anasteria
"What changed when you found us?" - Player
"Human ingenuity...and greed. Your base desires to seek out power are only matched by your ability to adapt and manifest that power in impressive displays. It was the reckless actions of the first
arcanists
that drew us to this world and it was our whispers that taught your people how to first tap into the limitless potential of the Aether. All it took was a push in the right direction. We had to be patient, but eventually enough of us were able to breach the veil and take matters into our own "hands" and prepare for
the Glorious Dawn
that would see all Aetherials return to the physical realm. But our actions were not without consequence. Though
Chthonian
worship was not a new prospect on
Cairn
, desperate humans made willing allies for the powers of
Ch'thon
and so an old enemy has returned to haunt us and may well spell doom for us all. Even now as we slaughter one another, the void feeds upon the spilled blood and grows to envelop all of creation." -
Anasteria
Possession
Aetherials have the ability to possess living creatures, notably including humans, taking control of the creature's body and mind. A creature possessed in this manner may be referred to as "vessel", and the survivors of
the Grim Dawn
referred to people lost in this manner as "Taken". This name was later used to refer to
a single person
who survived possession and became a key character in the resistance efforts against Aetherials and other forces endangering the humans of
Cairn
.
Possessed humans may or may not be distinguishable from those not hosting an Aetherial. Some vessels, human and not, have had their bodies distorted to various extent, which is immediately recognizable. Some humans have only the distinctively non-human voice and the bluish-green aether-colored glow coming from their eyes. Some others are just like ordinary humans in looks and voice, and detecting them as Aetherials requires unusual means, such as the presence of another Aetherial-possessed human.
An unusual and unanticipated (by Aetherials) side effect of human possession is that the Aetherial is influenced by the mind and emotions of its host. Such influence may even affect the Aetherial's motivation to the extent it chooses to side with the human resistance against the Aetherial invading force. At least two such cases are known.
Anasteria
, though highly distrusted due to her demeanor and use of controversial magic, provided valuable intelligence to
the Black Legion
.
Korinia
eventually became the leader of
the Malmouth resistance
.
Apparently, negative characteristics of the host's mind affect Aetherials as well.
Warden Krieg
certainly retained some of his behavior back from his time as the
Devil's Crossing
warden (for which he was admonished by his Aetherial superiors), and
Theodin Marcell
's creation of the
Fleshworks
is likely influenced by his former work as a surgeon.
An even more unusual and unanticipated side effect of human possession are those related to that possession is, in rare cases, reversible. After
Sahdina
,
Jarvis
, and
John Bourbon
interrogated a "Taken" that passed through the
riftgate
to Devil's Crossing, they hanged it. The Aetherial fled its vessel, and Bourbon had the human cut down. The "Taken" appears to have retained no memory of their past life, but some connection to the Aether remained:
Kasparov
could detect residual Aether energies within their body, and more notably, the Taken could capture riftgates and repurpose them for human use, as well as open personal riftgates at will to transport themself and other humans. This became a critical factor in the resistance's successes that followed: captured riftgates were safer than other means of transportation, whether just due to overwhelming enemy presence or due to a need to take a path through
the Void
to bypass aetherfire-scorched land.

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ancient Shambler
CATEGORIES: Boss Creatures, Eldritch
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Ancient_Shambler
======================================================================

The
Ancient Shambler
is a unique
Rock Golem
boss creature located in
The Flooded Passage
. Killing it is necessary to exit to the
Burrwitch Outskirts
.
Family:
Eldritch
Genus:
Golem

Ascended Attendant
Race
Aetherial
Type
Common
Faction
Aetherial Vanguard
Ascended Attendants
are a type of
Aetherial
creature, found throughout
Act 6
. On death they will spawn an
Aetherial Wraith
.

Damage Type

Melee
100% Weapon Damage
Elemental Damage

100% Chance to pass through Enemies

Ascended Attendant
Race
Aetherial
Type
Common
Faction
Aetherial Vanguard
Ascended Attendants
are a type of
Aetherial
creature, found throughout
Act 6
. On death they will spawn an
Aetherial Wraith
.
Abilities
Skill Name
Skill Type
Damage Type
Passive
Physical Damage
5% Chance of +35% Physical Damage
Elemental Damage
Increases Energy Regeneration by 50%
22% Elemental Resistance
Elemental Bolt
Projectile
1 Projectile
1.5 Meter Radius
Elemental Damage
30 Reduced target's Elemental Resistances for 3 Seconds
Elemental Bolt Nova
Projectile Ring
10 Projectile(s)
1.5 Meter Radius
Elemental Damage
Burn Damage over 2 Seconds
Resistances
Fire
20%
Cold
20%
Lightning
20%
Aether
30%
Freeze
25%
Petrify
25%

Ascended Myrmidon
Race
Aetherial
Type
Champion
Faction
Aetherial Vanguard
Ascended Myrmidons
are a type of
Aetherial
creature, found throughout
Act 6
. On death they will spawn an
Aetherial Wraith
.

buff

Ascended Spellbreaker
Race
Aetherial
Type
Champion
Faction
Aetherial Vanguard
Ascended Spellbreakers
are a type of
Aetherial
creature, found throughout
Act 6
. On death they will spawn an
Aetherial Wraith
.

Melee
100% Weapon Damage
Cold Damage
Frostburn Damage over 3 Seconds
30% Slow target for 3 Seconds
Frostbolt Nova
Projectile Ring
12 Projectile(s)
Cold Damage
Frostburn Damage over 2 Seconds

(Cast on Death)

======================================================================
PAGE: Ballog'Nath, the Hellfire
CATEGORIES: Boss Creatures, Chthonics
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Ballog'Nath,_the_Hellfire
======================================================================

Ballog'Nath, the Hellfire
is a unique
Boss
creature that can be found either in the
Buried Crypt
or the Rover Camp in the
Rotting Croplands
,  depending on the player's actions when they are talking to
Kalista
. Killing Ballog'Nath is necessary to complete the
Trapped and Alone
quest.
Family:
Chthonic
Genus:
Void Fiend
Abilities:
Warp Breath
Warp Stomp
Enrage
Void Fire
Ring of Flame
Mark of Pain
Resistances
Level 4
Hero/Boss Resistances
Chaos Resistance 25%
Fire Resistance 75%
Notable Drops
Masteries

======================================================================
PAGE: Balok'Anatu, Bloodfiend
CATEGORIES: Boss Creatures, Chthonics
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Balok'Anatu,_Bloodfiend
======================================================================

Balok'Anatu, Bloodfiend
is a unique
Boss
creature that can be found in the
Bastion of Chaos
.
He is guarding the way to the second level:
Discord
.
Family:
Chthonic
Genus:
Void Leech
Abilities
Charge
Life Drain
Challenging Roar
Double Swipe
Devouring Nova
Chaos Wave
Resistances
Level 4
Hero/Boss Resistances
Physical: 15%
Pierce: 15%
Chaos: 25%
Vitality: 100%
Stun: 50%
Notable Drops
Supreme Spoils
Blood of Ch'thon
Masteries

======================================================================
PAGE: Bane'Galgalor, Guardian of the Gate
CATEGORIES: Boss Creatures, Chthonics
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Bane'Galgalor,_Guardian_of_the_Gate
======================================================================

Bane'Galgalor, Guardian of the Gate
is a unique
Boss
creature that can be found in the
Tomb of the Watchers
.
He is guarding the path to the
Seal of the Loghorrean
.
Family:
Chthonic
Genus:
Void Leech
Abilities
Poison Strike
Poison Cloud
Vile Aura
Vile Spit
Resistances
Level 4
Hero/Boss Resistances
Physical: 15%
Fire: 50%
Vitality: 100%
Poison: 100%
Freeze: 500%
Knockdown: 500%
Petrify: 500%
Sleep: 500%
Stun: 500%
Trap: 500%
Notable Drops
Grand Spoils
Masteries

======================================================================
PAGE: Bane'Gargoth
CATEGORIES: Boss Creatures, Chthonics
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Bane'Gargoth
======================================================================

Bane'Gargoth
is a unique
Boss
creature that can be found in the
Obsidian Throne
. He is the target of
Braving the Void
quest.
Family:
Chthonic
Genus:
Obsidian Ravager
Abilities
Impale
Flame Wave
Flame Core
Fireball Nova Barrage
Resistances
Level 4
Hero/Boss Resistances
Physical: 10%
Pierce: 25%
Fire: 30%
Chaos: 25%
Vitality: 50%
Petrify: 500%
Sleep: 500%
Stun: 500%
Knockdown: 500%
Notable Drops
Supreme Spoils
Masteries

======================================================================
PAGE: Barrowholm (Faction)
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Barrowholm_(Faction)
======================================================================

Barrowholm, a veritable jewel in the mud, offers a stark contrast to the dangers of the surrounding bog with its nearly immaculate structures and peaceful existence. Its denizens are rightfully protective of their home and offer an unusual mix of caution and warm hospitality to new visitors.
After all, in a world ravaged by Aetherials and Chthonians, something this perfect must be protected at all costs.
Barrowholm
is one of the
Factions
in Grim Dawn. Just as with
The Outcast
, it is possible to make this faction either an allied or an enemy one, depending on the choices made when meeting their members during
Act 5
.
Headquarters:
Ugdenbog

======================================================================
PAGE: Benn'Jhar, the Colossal
CATEGORIES: Chthonics, Nemesis
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Benn'Jhar,_the_Colossal
======================================================================

Benn'Jahr, the Colossal
is a
Nemesis Spawn
of the
Chthonians
's faction.
He is an Ultra Boss of Grim Dawn so the player must be prepared for that encounter.
Family:
Chthonians
Genus:
Obsidian Ravager
Spawn Points
The Blood Grove
(Elite and Ultimate)
Village of Darkvale
(Elite and Ultimate)
Darkvale Gate
(Elite and Ultimate)
Sorrow's Bastion
Ashen Waste
Tomb of the Watchers
Depraved Sanctuary
The Forsaken Wastes
Obsidian Throne
Bastion of Chaos
Discord
Anarchy
Entropy
Void's Edge
Cinder Wastes
Mourndale
Abilities
Chaos Stomp
Impale
Obsidian Prison
Summon Cluster
Enrage
Warp Breath
Resistances
Level 5 Hero/Boss Resistances
50% Chaos Resistance
500% Reduced Knockdown Duration
20% Physical Resistance
20% Pierce Resistance
50% Poison & Acid Resistance
Notable Drops
Benn'Jahr's Pauldrons
Benn'Jahr's Shoulderguards

======================================================================
PAGE: Bloodsworn
CATEGORIES: Chthonics
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Bloodsworn
======================================================================

For the enemy faction, see
Chthonians
.
Bloodsworn are a class of enemy
creature
. They are the
human
members of the Cult of Ch'thon, and aligned with the
Chthonians
Faction.
Quotes
"The Ch'thonic cults have been around since before the
first traveler
took to the roads. Under the Empire, they were outlawed and driven underground. Where cults were uncovered by inquisitors, the Emperor's
nightblades
would be sent to hunt down and execute the practitioners. Even so, they could not be eradicated and now they grow ever more powerful as people flock to them seeking purpose or salvation. More troubling though, I've heard tale that the cults have had success of late in calling forth the monstrous servants of their god to walk this world." -
Kuldo
"Have you run into the Bloodsworn yet? The fanatical cult devoted to the supposed elder god
Ch'thon
? The cult has existed forever; some say since before recorded history. I always thought the myth of an elder god betrayed and gruesomely dismembered was too fantastical to be anything but the stuff of theatre but since the Grim Dawn, it seems the cult has newfound power and desperate men flock to swell their ranks." -
Ulgrim
"The Cult of Ch'thon? Not a bunch of people you want to cross. Obsessed with the blood of some fallen god, or maybe it's just blood in general. Wasn't 'til I attended one of their underground meetings that I realized I was mixing with the wrong crowd; and believe me, I've been affiliated with some pretty twisted individuals before. Ran out of there before they were done with the first 'initiate'. You don't want to know what that entails." -
Direni
"Though Chthonian worship was not a new prospect on Cairn, desperate humans made willing allies for the powers of Ch'thon and so an old enemy has returned to haunt us and may well spell doom for us all.Even now as we slaughter one another, the void feeds upon the spilled blood and grows to envelop all of creation." -
Anasteria
Bloodsworn Variants

======================================================================
PAGE: Bounty
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Bounty
======================================================================

Bounties are small quests which allow the player to increase faction reputation, and are offered by the various friendly
Factions
at a
Bounty Table
.
There are a total of 145 bounties split between the game’s 7 friendly factions. Bounty tasks can take players all over the game world and vary from finding or crafting particular items to slaying Bosses, Heroes or other monsters. There are 35 Hero monsters that spawn exclusively through the bounty system.
The
Ashes of Malmouth
Expansion adds 3 more friendly Factions, with another 42 bounties and 16 exclusive hero creatures.
Forgotten Gods
also adds 3 new factions, but the 16 new bounties are shared and grant Reputation to all three.
General Information
Bounties are unguided, with minimal instructions and no quest markers on the mini-map.
There is a maximum of five bounties per faction per game session.
Players can have one bounty from each faction active at any given time.
Bounties can still be undertaken after reaching maximum reputation.
In addition to the reputation reward for completing a bounty, killing bosses and heroes can grant bonus reputation.
Killing an enemy prior to accepting a bounty on them does not complete the bounty.
Default Enemies do not respawn for bounties. If a target has already been slain during the game session, it will be necessary to restart.
Bounty reputation rewards are increased if a character has a
writ
(+50%) or
mandate
(+150%) from the relevant Faction.

are a type of creature belonging to the
Chthonic
race.
They come in three main variations.

Chthonian Devourer
Race
Chthonic
Type
Champion
Faction
Chthonians
The Ch'thonian Devourer is an abomination aptly named for its insatiable propensity to consume anything and everything it comes across. While physically weak, Devourers rely on numbers and devilishly quick movements to take down their prey.
Chthonian Devourers
are a type of
Chthonic
creature.
Abilities
Passive
Physical Damage
Poison Damage
Megachomp

Chthonian Drone
Race
Chthonic
Type
Common
Faction
Chthonians
Chthonian Drones
are a type of
Chthonic
creature found in Void areas of
Act 5
and
Act 6
.
Abilities
Skill Name
Skill Type
Damage Type
Passive
Physical Damage
Generic Charge
Charge
(low speed)
5 Charge Levels: +15%, +30%, +45%, +60%, +75% to All Damage
100% Weapon Damage
+30% Movement Speed
+50% Attack Speed
+50% Casting Speed
Impale
Melee
Piercing Damage
Vitality Damage
Bleeding Damage over 2 Seconds

Pierce
25%
Poison & Acid
25%
Knockdown
500%

25%

25%

Servitor's Slicer

Chthonian Gorger
Race
Chthonic
Type
Champion
Faction
Chthonians
Chthonian Gorgers
are a type of
Chthonic
creature.
Abilities
Passive
Physical Damage
Acid Damage
Acid Vomit
3 Second Cooldown
Acid Damage
Poison Damage over 5 Seconds
50% Chance to Fumble Attacks for 3 Seconds
50% Chance to Fumble Projectiles for 3 Seconds
Resistances
Fire: 25%
Chaos: 25%
Vitality: 50%
Gallery
Masteries

Chthonian Harvester
Race
Chthonic
Type
Champion
Faction
Chthonians
Chthonian Harvesters
are a type of
Chthonic
creature found in Void areas of
Act 5
and
Act 6
.

Chthonian Hungerer
Race
Chthonic
Type
Common
Faction
Chthonians
Chthonian Hungerers
are a type of
Chthonic
creature.

Chomp
Melee
Life Leech
Resistances

Chaos: 15%
Vitality: 30%

Type
Common
Faction

are a type of
Chthonic
creature.

======================================================================
PAGE: Chthonians
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Chthonians
======================================================================

Chthonians, horrors from the Void, are the things of nightmares. Prior to the Grim Dawn, Chthonians would only be seen upon Cairn following an arcane ritual gone wrong or at the beckoning of a powerful sorcerer.
Despite an effort to exterminate the practice, a cult has formed around blood rituals and the worship of Chthonic beings. Members of the cult have been overheard chanting one name in particular: Ch'thon. With the world in disarray, the cult has grown strong, garnering new followers desperate for answers in a world that no longer makes sense.
Chthonians, and their loyal cult, appear to be at odds with the Aetherials, eagerly harvesting helpless humans for blood so that the Aetherials cannot use them as vessels.
The
Ch'thonians
are one of two races of otherworldly entities present in the Grim Dawn universe and make up one of the various enemy
Factions
present in the game.
Killing
Chthonic
and
Bloodsworn
enemies will increase the players' negative Faction Reputation.
Quotes
Bloodsworn
"The Ch'thonic cults have been around since before the
first traveler
took to the roads. Under the Empire, they were outlawed and driven underground. Where cults were uncovered by inquisitors, the Emperor's
nightblades
would be sent to hunt down and execute the practitioners. Even so, they could not be eradicated and now they grow ever more powerful as people flock to them seeking purpose or salvation. More troubling though, I've heard tale that the cults have had success of late in calling forth the monstrous servants of their god to walk this world." -
Kuldo
"Have you run into the Bloodsworn yet? The fanatical cult devoted to the supposed elder god
Ch'thon
? The cult has existed forever; some say since before recorded history. I always thought the myth of an elder god betrayed and gruesomely dismembered was too fantastical to be anything but the stuff of theatre but since the Grim Dawn, it seems the cult has newfound power and desperate men flock to swell their ranks." -
Ulgrim
"The Cult of Ch'thon? Not a bunch of people you want to cross. Obsessed with the blood of some fallen god, or maybe it's just blood in general. Wasn't 'til I attended one of their underground meetings that I realized I was mixing with the wrong crowd; and believe me, I've been affiliated with some pretty twisted individuals before. Ran out of there before they were done with the first 'initiate'. You don't want to know what that entails." -
Direni
"Though Chthonian worship was not a new prospect on Cairn, desperate humans made willing allies for the powers of Ch'thon and so an old enemy has returned to haunt us and may well spell doom for us all.Even now as we slaughter one another, the void feeds upon the spilled blood and grows to envelop all of creation." -
Anasteria

======================================================================
PAGE: Councilman Garl
CATEGORIES: Aetherials, Boss Creatures
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Councilman_Garl
======================================================================

Councilman Garl
Race
Aether Corruption
Type
Boss
Faction
Aetherials
Councilman Garl
is a final Boss creature for
Port Valbury
. He spawns together with
Overlord Van Aldritch
and
Councilman Hallos
.
The three pass around Aldritch's Scepter, indicated by an icon above their head. Whichever holds the scepter gains the
Scepter Storm
buff and the
Aetherbolt Nova
skill.
Aditionally, On death, Councilman Garl confers another buff on remaining allies (see below).
Abilities
Scepter Storm
40% Damage Absorption
+40/60% Total Damage
+20/50% Life Reegneration
Scepter Aetherbolt Nova
8 Projectiles in a 360 Degree Radius
Aether Damage
Fire Damage
Burn Damage over 5 Seconds
30% Total Resistance Reduction for 5 seconds
1 second of Stun
Aether Shard
1 Projectile
3 Meter Explosion Radius
5 Second Duration
Aether Damage
Aether Burst
18 Meter Range
Aether Damage
Burn Damage over 5 Seconds
Reduced Defensive Ability for 5 Seconds
Aethershield
Casts on 20% Life
12 Second Duration
90 Second Cooldown
70% Damage Absorption
+60% Total Damage
Aether Zap
Aether Damage
Vitality Damage
35% reduced Total Speed for 2 seconds
Poison Nova
12 Projectiles in a 360 Degree Radius
Physical Damage
Acid Damage
Poison Damage over 2 Seconds
30% Total Resistance Reduction for 5 seconds
1 second of Stun
Poison Fumes
6 Meter Radius
6 Second Duration
Poison Damage
25% Chance to Fumble for 2 Seconds
-15% Resistances for 2 Seconds
Death Buff
50 Meter Radius
600 second Duration
+80% Total Damage
+15% Crit Damage
+35% Cast Speed
+5% Offensive Ability
+4% Defensive Ability
12% Damage Absorption
20% Skill Cooldown Reduction
Resistances
Level 4
Hero/Boss Resistances
Physical: 30%
Pierce: 35%
Lightning: 33%
Knockdown: 500%

======================================================================
PAGE: Councilman Hallos
CATEGORIES: Aetherials, Boss Creatures
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Councilman_Hallos
======================================================================

Councilman Hallos
Race
Aether Corruption
Type
Boss
Faction
Aetherials
Councilman Hallos
is a final Boss creature for
Port Valbury
. He spawns together with
Overlord Van Aldritch
and
Councilman Garl
.
The three pass around Aldritch's Scepter, indicated by an icon above their head. Whichever holds the scepter gains the
Scepter Storm
buff and the
Aetherbolt Nova
skill.
Aditionally, On death, Councilman Hallos confers another buff on remaining allies (see below).
Abilities
Scepter Storm
40% Damage Absorption
+40/60% Total Damage
+20/50% Life Reegneration
Scepter Aetherbolt Nova
8 Projectiles in a 360 Degree Radius
Aether Damage
Fire Damage
Burn Damage over 5 Seconds
30% Total Resistance Reduction for 5 seconds
1 second of Stun
Aether Shard
1 Projectile
3 Meter Explosion Radius
5 Second Duration
Aether Damage
Aether Burst
18 Meter Range
Aether Damage
Burn Damage over 5 Seconds
Reduced Defensive Ability for 5 Seconds
Aethershield
Casts on 20% Life
12 Second Duration
90 Second Cooldown
70% Damage Absorption
+60% Total Damage
Summon Aether Trap
Trap lives for 45 Seconds
5 Meter radius
Aether Damage
Burn Damage over 2 Seconds
Stun for 1-2.5 seconds
Blizzard
6 Second Duration
Physical Damage
Cold Damage
Frostburn Damage over 2 Seconds
-30% Total Speed for 5 Seconds
Howling Wind
5 Second Duration
Cold Damage
-25% Total Speed for 2 Seconds
Death Buff
50 Meter Radius
600 second Duration
+80% Total Damage
+15% Crit Damage
+35% Cast Speed
+5% Offensive Ability
+4% Defensive Ability
12% Damage Absorption
20% Skill Cooldown Reduction
Resistances
Level 4
Hero/Boss Resistances
Physical: 30%
Pierce: 35%
Lightning: 33%
Knockdown: 500%

======================================================================
PAGE: Coven of Ugdenbog
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Coven_of_Ugdenbog
======================================================================

Not much is known about the mysterious Coven of Ugdenbog. A congregation of witches and warlocks, the coven seeks to shield its persectued brothers and sisters from a society that does not understand and thus (often rightfully) fears their powers.
Having successfully evaded the pursuit of the empire's Luminari for decades, the witches created a place of respite deep within the treacherous Ugdenbog, a place not even thier determined pursuers dare to tread. This comes with its own dangers however.
Those who found refuge with the coven must remain ever vigilant as new threats, whether from lost travelers stumbling upon their hideout or from the deadly wildlife of the bog itself, continue to arise.
Worse still, there are secrets within the bog which even the coven avoids, lest mistakes of the past be repeated and the Luminari rediscover their trail.
The
Coven of Ugdenbog
is one of the allied
Factions
in Grim Dawn. Members of this faction are first met in
Act 5
when playing the
Ashes of Malmouth
Expansion.

======================================================================
PAGE: Cronley's Gang
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Cronley's_Gang
======================================================================

Unlike most groups of roaming bandits, Cronley's Gang appears to be highly organized and heavily armed, answering only to their self-proclaimed leader, Darius Cronley.
Having escaped Devil's Crossing Prison following the chaos that ensued after the Grim Dawn, these criminals are now intent on taking the one place in the world that once denied them their freedom. Members of Cronley's Gang are sadistic, violent and prone to acts of aggression against their fellow humans, epitomizing that perhaps the greatest threat to humanity is humanity itself.
Cronley's Gang are a class of
human
enemy and make up one of the enemy
Factions
in Grim Dawn. They are confined to
Act 2
areas:
Arkovian Foothills
,
Old Arkovia
, the
Four Hills
and
Cronley's Hideout
.
Faction Rewards
Killing Cronley's Gang members will increase the players' negative Faction Reputation.
Faction Status
Reward
Hostile:
none

Constellations

======================================================================
PAGE: Cult of Bysmiel
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Cult_of_Bysmiel
======================================================================

Worshipping the elusive Lady of Whispers, the Cult of Bysmiel prides itself on manipulation and subterfuge.
It was by Bysmiel's machinations that the empire's inquisitors spent centuries aimlessly hunting for the Witch God cults, their efforts confounded by the endless weave of decoys and lies.
All this is made possible through a vast network of spies and informants, known as Bysmiel's web. Only a select few are trusted in knowing the full span of Bysmiel's schemes.
The Cult of
Bysmiel
is an allied
Faction
within Grim Dawn
Forgotten Gods
.
Headquarters:
Conclave of the Three
Reputation gained from:
killing
Eldritch Horrors
Quests
Bounties
Lore
The Witch Gods are said to wield unfathomable powers. Their followers worship them in the hopes of getting a mere taste of this vast potential. Naturally, the Erulan emperors did not approve of such heresy and the Luminari were dispatched to exterminate any that defy the commands of the throne and the faith of Empyrion.
But the Witch Gods are clever, their covens well hidden. For centuries, their worshippers have evaded capture. They worked from the shadows, secretly manipulating the empire’s citizens and growing their numbers.
When the Grim Dawn struck, all of humanity paid the price for the hubris of the Gildam Arcanum arcanists, the Witch God cults included. Yet it was their efforts to evade the Luminari that spared them the brunt of the initial carnage. The cults receded to their hidden places to tend to their wounds and recover their strength. All the while, the Witch Gods plotted their next move...
But cosmic forces are at play that go beyond just the conflict between the Aetherials and the Chthonians. Forgotten powers are stirring in the celestial vale. Powers that threaten even the Witch Gods themselves. And so, in an unprecedented move, the cults have gathered as one. No longer divided by their devotion, they became unified in a singular purpose: to halt the return of the cataclysm that ravaged the Korvan lands.

======================================================================
PAGE: Cult of Dreeg
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Cult_of_Dreeg
======================================================================

Bestowed true sight by the Great Guardian, the worshippers of Dreeg are consumed by visions of possible futures. Many are driven mad by the whispers, but those that endure become invaluable assets capable of altering future events in the pursuit of the Witch Gods' goals.
Dreeg's worshippers say that to stand before the Great Guardian is a blessing without match. His words of wisdom echo through the eons as he seeks to strike a balance in the tumultuous strands of fate woven by the celestials themselves. It is said that none can witness Dreeg's warped form in person without their feeble mortal mind shattering.
The Cult of
Dreeg
are an allied
Faction
within Grim Dawn
Forgotten Gods
.
Headquarters:
Conclave of the Three
Reputation gained from:
killing
Eldritch Horrors
Quests
Bounties
Lore
The Witch Gods are said to wield unfathomable powers. Their followers worship them in the hopes of getting a mere taste of this vast potential. Naturally, the Erulan emperors did not approve of such heresy and the Luminari were dispatched to exterminate any that defy the commands of the throne and the faith of Empyrion.
But the Witch Gods are clever, their covens well hidden. For centuries, their worshippers have evaded capture. They worked from the shadows, secretly manipulating the empire’s citizens and growing their numbers.
When the Grim Dawn struck, all of humanity paid the price for the hubris of the Gildam Arcanum arcanists, the Witch God cults included. Yet it was their efforts to evade the Luminari that spared them the brunt of the initial carnage. The cults receded to their hidden places to tend to their wounds and recover their strength. All the while, the Witch Gods plotted their next move...
But cosmic forces are at play that go beyond just the conflict between the Aetherials and the Chthonians. Forgotten powers are stirring in the celestial vale. Powers that threaten even the Witch Gods themselves. And so, in an unprecedented move, the cults have gathered as one. No longer divided by their devotion, they became unified in a singular purpose: to halt the return of the cataclysm that ravaged the Korvan lands.

======================================================================
PAGE: Cult of Solael
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Cult_of_Solael
======================================================================

The very essence of the Eldritch Realm is for Solael to mold as he sees fit, and he shares this knowledge with his loyal servants. But such power does not come without sacrifice. Some must die so that others can bask in his glory, the ultimate display of loyalty.
The worshippers of Solael would pay any price to taste of Solael's gifts. They say that the Eldritch Fire is without equal and worth spilling a little blood, even if it has to be the blood of their own...or their allies.
The Cult of
Solael
is an allied
Faction
within Grim Dawn
Forgotten Gods
.
Headquarters:
Conclave of the Three
Reputation gained from:
killing
Eldritch Horrors
Quests
Bounties
Lore
The Witch Gods are said to wield unfathomable powers. Their followers worship them in the hopes of getting a mere taste of this vast potential. Naturally, the Erulan emperors did not approve of such heresy and the Luminari were dispatched to exterminate any that defy the commands of the throne and the faith of Empyrion.
But the Witch Gods are clever, their covens well hidden. For centuries, their worshippers have evaded capture. They worked from the shadows, secretly manipulating the empire’s citizens and growing their numbers.
When the Grim Dawn struck, all of humanity paid the price for the hubris of the Gildam Arcanum arcanists, the Witch God cults included. Yet it was their efforts to evade the Luminari that spared them the brunt of the initial carnage. The cults receded to their hidden places to tend to their wounds and recover their strength. All the while, the Witch Gods plotted their next move...
But cosmic forces are at play that go beyond just the conflict between the Aetherials and the Chthonians. Forgotten powers are stirring in the celestial vale. Powers that threaten even the Witch Gods themselves. And so, in an unprecedented move, the cults have gathered as one. No longer divided by their devotion, they became unified in a singular purpose: to halt the return of the cataclysm that ravaged the Korvan lands.

======================================================================
PAGE: Devil's Crossing (faction)
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Devil's_Crossing_(faction)
======================================================================

This article is about the faction. For the location it currently inhabits, see
Devil's Crossing
.
Survivors... that is the word used most often to describe the hardy denizens of
Devil's Crossing
. The men and women who made it through the
Grim Dawn
to form a small community within the ruins of an abandoned prison. They have seen all too many friends and family lost to the "Taken"; those found possessed by the
Aetherials
.
Earning their trust will prove no easy task, but with supplies running low and a looming Aetherial threat on the horizon, they may just be willing to take a chance.
Devil's Crossing
is an allied
faction
in Grim Dawn.
Headquarters:
Devil's Crossing
Reputation gained from:
Killing
Cronley's Gang

Bounties

======================================================================
PAGE: Eldritch Horrors
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Eldritch_Horrors
======================================================================

This article is a
stub
. You can help Grim Dawn Wiki by
expanding it
.
This article is a
stub
. You can help Grim Dawn Wiki by
expanding it
.
Eldritch creatures were capable of entering Cairn through tears in the veil for centuries, but in the Korvan lands the barrier between realities seems to be at its weakest. Here the terrors are numerous and their forms unlike any seen in other parts of Cairn. They strike with tooth and claw teeming with Eldritch energies and swoop down on unsuspecting prey upon armored wings.
Eldritch Horrors
are the only hostile faction in the
Korvan Plateau
.
Quotes
Faction Rewards
Faction Status
Reward
Hostile:
none
Despised:
Extra Spawns
Hated:
Additional Hero Spawns
Nemesis:
Nemesis Spawns
The Eldritch Nemesis is
Kaisan, the Eldritch Scion
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Faction quartermaster
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Faction_quartermaster
======================================================================

This article is a
stub
. You can help Grim Dawn Wiki by
expanding it
.
This article is a
stub
. You can help Grim Dawn Wiki by
expanding it
.
Faction quartermasters
are merchants who sell faction-specific gear,
blueprints
,
consumables
and faction special items (such as
writs
and
mandates
). To access the stock of a faction quartermaster, the player needs to gain some reputation with the faction, with higher reputation levels unlocking more of the stock.
List of quartermasters
For
The Black Legion
:
Thalia in
Homestead
Klines in
Fort Ikon
For
the Devil's Crossing faction
:
Ellis in
Devil's Crossing
For
the Homestead faction
:
Whitfield in
Homestead
For
Kymon's Chosen
:
Brother Adrius in
Kymon's Sanctuary
For
Order of Death's Vigil
:
Keeper Malvor in the
Bastion of the Order
For
Rovers
:
Keeper Unkala in
Old Arkovia
For
The Outcast
:
Anasteria
's Drudge in the
Fort Ikon Prison
For
Coven of Ugdenbog
:
Falonestra at the
Coven's Refuge
For
the Barrowholm faction
Igrid Galstrom in
Barrowholm
For
Malmouth Resistance
:
Ronan Karkan in the
Malmouth Sewers
Plaudia in the
Steelcap District

======================================================================
PAGE: Factions
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Factions
======================================================================

This article is a
stub
. You can help Grim Dawn Wiki by
expanding it
.
This article is a
stub
. You can help Grim Dawn Wiki by
expanding it
.
The
faction system
in
Grim Dawn
allows the player to take advantage of the reputation (and infamy) gained with the various groups of
Cairn
. Completing
quests
, turning in
bounties
, and defeating members of opposing factions earns reputation with friendly factions, which gives access to perks such as
merchant
discounts, special equipment from
quartermasters
, and new side quests. Killing members of hostile factions earns infamy with these factions, which makes more enemies of that faction spawn, and later grants access to
Nemesis
enemies.
Factions
The base game features 12 factions, of which at the start of a game 7 are friendly and 5 are hostile. The
Ashes of Malmouth
and
Forgotten Gods
expansions each add three friendly and one hostile faction, bringing the number of factions to 20.
Some initially friendly factions may become hostile depending on player choice.
Allied factions
The Black Legion
Devil's Crossing
Homestead
Kymon's Chosen
Order of Death's Vigil
Rovers

AoM

FG
Cult of Dreeg
FG
Cult of Solael
FG
Hostile factions
Aetherials
Beasts
Chthonians
Cronley's Gang
Arkovian Undead
Aetherial Vanguard
AoM

FG
Faction reputation
The faction window
Depending on whether a faction is friendly or hostile, the player can gain either positive or negative reputation with it. In some cases (such as when turning in some quests), gained reputation can be lost, but this cannot make a friendly faction hostile even if the reputation with that faction would go below 0 (it is set to 0 instead). The following actions result in reputation changes:
Turning in
quests
and
bounties
.
The player gains positive reputation with the faction that benefits from the quest (usually, but not always, the one that gains the quest).
If this quest was aimed against a hostile faction, the player may gain infamy with that faction.
If this quest harmed another friendly faction, the player may lose reputation with that faction.
Defeating enemies.
If the enemy was a member of a hostile faction, the player gains infamy with that faction.
If some friendly factions have this hostile faction listed as an enemy, the player gains reputation with these friendly faction. This can only work if the faction is "known" to the character, which typically requires them to complete a quest for that faction on any difficulty.
Reputation changes are affected by what kind of enemy is defeated. Heroes, bosses, and
nemeses
grant more reputation that regular enemies. Enemies of any kind with a level far below that of the player don't affect reputation at all.
There are two sets of reputation tiers that form the progressions for friendly and hostile factions. Reaching higher tiers with a faction grants perks that are listed below.
Friendly faction reputation tiers:
Tolerated (0 to 1,499)
Friendly (1,500 to 4,999)
Respected (5,000 to 9,999)
Honored (10,000 to 24,999)
Revered (25,000)
Hostile faction reputation tiers:
Hostile (1 to 1,499)
Despised (1,500 to 7,999)
Additional champions start spawning, sometimes with monsters that do not normally spawn in an area appearing to bolster their forces.
Hated (8,000 to 19,999)
Heroes of that faction start spawning more often wherever their forces are found. In the case of
Kymon's Chosen
and the
Order of Death's Vigil
, this will be the first time their heroes are encountered.
Nemesis (20,000)
Nemesis
enemies of that faction start spawning in that faction's territory. Nemeses are rare and very powerful bosses, but they drop more loot and are more likely to drop rare
blueprints
.
Bounties
Factions offer
bounties
via their respective bounty tables. These are small quests where the player can earn additional rewards and reputation. There are a total of 145 bounty missions across the 7 friendly factions.
Faction equipment
Each friendly faction offers its own specific equipment for purchase, available from the
faction quartermasters
as rewards for gaining reputation.
Reputation items
Grim Dawn
features items that amplify reputation gains with factions.
Writs
A writ is a consumable item that boosts a character's reputation gain with its respective faction by 50%. Unlike mandates, writs are soulbound, i. e. they cannot be transferred. Even though it's possible to purchase multiple writs from a single faction's
quartermaster
, only one writ per faction per character can actually be used.
Mandates
A mandate is a consumable item that boosts a character's reputation gain with its respective faction by 200%. Mandates cannot be used by the character that buys them since they are already Revered with that faction so place them in the transfer stash for other characters to use. Even though it's possible to purchase multiple mandates from a single faction's quartermaster, only one mandate per faction per character can actually be used.
Warrants
Nemesis
bosses have a chance to drop warrants, which boost infamy gained with the respective faction by 50% across all difficulties. Warrants can be freely traded between players.
Faction choices
There are some considerations that apply whenever the game gives the option to become hostile with a previously-friendly faction. Such a choice locks out that faction's quests and bounties, their
quartermaster
, and makes writs and mandates for that faction unavailable. It also makes that faction available as an enemy, meaning the ability to earn items (including
monster infrequents
), experience, reputation, and to defeat their
nemesis
, which also gives access to warrants for that faction. As such, it may be useful to have separate characters to get the benefits of all choices (which is time-consuming), or at least to take different sides on different difficulty levels (which, hovewer, has its own downsides such as reputation resets). There also may be moral or roleplay points made for or against specific choices.
The subsections below describe the specifics of each individual choice in more detail and do not offer the generic advice listed above.
Kymon's Chosen vs Death's Vigil
See:
Kymon's Chosen
,
Order of Death's Vigil
Each faction has their faction vendor items to consider. TODO: describe.
TODO: Quest asymmetry...
The choice affects the events of the
Forgotten Gods
expansion slightly. TODO: describe.
With or against the Outcast
See:
The Outcast
Siding with
Anasteria
will make it more difficult to earn reputation with
the Black Legion
. In addition, Anasteria provides a significant amount of information about the game's lore and backstory if left alive, and provides even more such knowledge in the
Ashes of Malmouth
expansion.
Siding with the Black Legion soldiers will give access to an additional dungeon and the ability to farm Anasteria for her unique drops.
With or against Barrowholm
See:
Barrowholm (Faction)
Choosing the diplomatic solution with Barrowholm gives the ability to fight two bosses: the celestial boss
Ravager
and the easily-repeated boss
Larria
.
Choosing to attack Barrowholm gives access to an additional dungeon and the
nemesis
,
Reaper of the Lost
.
Witch cult choice
See:
Cult of Bysmiel
,
Cult of Dreeg
,
Cult of Solael
Unlike with the other choices, the factions not chosen do not become hostile, so there is no loss of items incurred. However, only the quests for the chosen faction are available, and while the main storyline quests have identical rewards, the Honored-tier side quests provide blueprints unique to both difficulty and faction. As such, it's required to use several characters to get all the rewards.
The Bysmiel cult may be the fastest one to play with: it's possible to get extra
Blood of the Watchers
for future difficulties, and its quests are less "out of the way" than the other factions. Its Honored-tier quest is also simpler than the other two.
Particular notes on each:
Cult of Bysmiel:
Main quest unique area:
Veiled Den
(Eldritch lands) with the boss
Galfang
, who does not have a unique drop.
Honored-tier quest:
Kymon's Deserters
, an easy "Find the NPCs" quest.
Korvan Swiftness
, a leaping and fire movement augment.
Cult of Dreeg:
Main quest unique area:
Edge of Reality
(Chthonic) with the boss
Ishtal
, who does not have a unique drop.
Honored-tier quest:
Future is Madness
, to kill the boss
Inarah
(no unique drop).
Dreeg's Vector
, a teleport and acid movement augment.
Cult of Solael:
Main quest unique area:
Blood Wastes
(Chthonic void) with the boss
Onarix
, who does not have a unique drop.
Honored-tier quest:
Word of Rahn
, finding and aligning statues in
Korvan City
.
Rahn's Might
, a shield-based movement augment.

Fleshwarped Scorcher
Race
Aetherial
Type
Champion
Faction
Aetherial Vanguard
Fleshwarped Scorchers
are a type of
Aetherial
creature, found throughout
Act 6
.

10% Chance to Avoid Projectiles

Melee auto-attack

2.5 Second Charge Duration
Physical Damage
Fire Damage
5-10% Reduction to Enemy's Health
Reduced target's Defensive Ability for 3 Seconds

Affects up to 3 targets

buff

4 Second Duration

is an
Aetherial
boss that appears in
Malmouth Harbor
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Fleshweaver Krieg
CATEGORIES: Aetherials, Boss Creatures
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Fleshweaver_Krieg
======================================================================

Fleshweaver Krieg
is a unique
Boss
Monster. Is found in the northwest corner of the
Fleshworks
, accessible from the
The Infestation
.
When spawning at level 94 or above, Fleshweaver Krieg can drop the Chestguard and Mask from the
Krieg's Armament
Legendary Set (4% chance for each). He is best farmed in
Elite
difficulty, as the drop chance is the same as in
Ultimate
.

expanding it
.
Galfang, Night Terror
is an
Eldritch
,
Beast
Boss located in the
Veiled Den
. He is the target of
The Power of Galfang
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Gargabol, Heart of the Wild
CATEGORIES: Boss Creatures, Eldritch
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Gargabol,_Heart_of_the_Wild
======================================================================

This article is a
stub
. You can help Grim Dawn Wiki by
expanding it
.
This article is a
stub
. You can help Grim Dawn Wiki by
expanding it
.
Gargabol
is a unique
Boss creature
. He is of the
Eldritch faction
. He is required to be killed in the quest
Cleanse the Corruption
. His boss arena has a unique name in the
Tainted Wood
called "Heart of the Forest". To challenge this boss you will need to bring a
Skeleton Key
.
It is a 2 phase boss fight. Phase 1 has Gargabol use acid/poison attacks. Once its HP is depleted, it revives as and begins to use fire attacks, in addition to spawning adds.

======================================================================
PAGE: Gazer Prime
CATEGORIES: Aetherials, Boss Creatures
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Gazer_Prime
======================================================================

This article is a
stub
. You can help Grim Dawn Wiki by
expanding it
.
This article is a
stub
. You can help Grim Dawn Wiki by
expanding it
.
Gazer Prime
is a secret Giant Gazer boss in
The Shattered Realm
in a shard that looks like its from the Fleshworks. He drops the
Void Orb
for the Hungerling
cosmetic pet
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Golem
CATEGORIES: Eldritch
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Golem
======================================================================

Golems are a
creature
type belonging to the
Eldritch
race. They take the form of giant animated rock creatures.
Golems possess relatively high Armor and Health, and have high Poison Resistance. They can be found in early
Act 2
areas.
Golem Variants
Earthen Shambler
Mossy Shambler
Stonewrought  Shambler
Ugdenbog Golem
Ugdenbog Mossflinger
Ugdenbog Overgrowth
Golem Heroes
Hammerton
Haldras Flamespitter
Stoneheart - Reflective
Stonegrinder - Charger
Rimestone - Frozen
Voidstone - Voidtouched
Ferrosius - Swift
Titanius Steelskin - Defender
Jormundur - Diseased
Garrl - Voidtouched
Golem Bosses
Ancient Shambler
Bargoll, the Mouldering Mound

======================================================================
PAGE: Guardian of Bysmiel
CATEGORIES: Boss Creatures, Eldritch
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Guardian_of_Bysmiel
======================================================================

The
Guardian of Bysmiel
is a unique
Boss
creature that can be encountered in
The Hidden Path
near
Asterkarn Road
.
The Player need the
Runestone of Dreeg
and
Runestone of Solael
to enter it's lair. Killing him is necessary for the quest as he drops the
Runestone of Bysmiel
upon death.
Family:
Eldritch
Abilities
Lightning Discharge
Storm Claw
Lightning Breath
Teleport Self
Chain Lightning
Curse
Resistances
Level 4
Hero/Boss Resistances
Chaos Resistance: 33%
Fire Resistance: 33%
Lightning Resistance: 50%
Physical Resistance: 25%
Pierce Resistance: 25%
Notable Drops
Grand Spoils
Runestone of Bysmiel
Note:
The Hidden Path - Bysmiel
Bysmiel-Sect Legguards
Masteries

======================================================================
PAGE: Guardian of Dreeg
CATEGORIES: Boss Creatures, Eldritch
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Guardian_of_Dreeg
======================================================================

The
Guardian of Dreeg
is a unique
Boss
creature that can be encountered in
The Hidden Path
near
Lower Crossing
. Killing him is necessary to launch the quest
The Hidden Path
. He also drops the
Runestone of Dreeg
upon death, mandatory for the quest.
Family:
Eldritch
Abilities
Poison Nova
Poison Spit
Gaze of Dreeg
Summon Guardian's Eye
Poison Spit Burst
Resistances
Level 4
Hero/Boss Resistances
Aether Resistance: 33%
Vitality Resistance: 33%
Physical Resistance: 25%
Pierce Resistance: 25%
Poison & Acid Resistance: 50%
Notable Drops
Grand Spoils
Runestone of Dreeg
Note:
The Hidden Path - Dreeg
Dreeg-Sect Legguards
Masteries

======================================================================
PAGE: Guardian of Solael
CATEGORIES: Boss Creatures, Eldritch
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Guardian_of_Solael
======================================================================

The
Guardian of Solael
is a unique
Boss
creature that can be found in
The Hidden Path
near the
Broken Hills
.
The player need the
Runestone of Dreeg
to enter it's lair. Killing him is necessary for the quest as he drops the
Runestone of Solael
upon death.
Family:
Eldritch
Abilities:
Life Leech
Bleeding
Vitality Stormwave
Siphoning Spirit
Doom Bolt
Resistances:
Level 4
Hero/Boss Resistances
Physical Resistance: 25%
Pierce Resistance: 25%
Cold Resistance: 33%
Aether Resistance: 33%
Vitality Resistance: 50%
Notable Drops
Grand Spoils
Runestone of Solael
Note:
The Hidden Path - Solael
Solael-Sect Legguards
Masteries
Items
Creatures
Factions
Locations
Constellations

======================================================================
PAGE: Herald of Destruction
CATEGORIES: Aetherials, Boss Creatures
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Herald_of_Destruction
======================================================================

The
Herald of Destruction
is a unique
Boss
creature that can be found in
The Immolation
. To enter the area the player must have the quest
Legion Retribution
however. He must be killed to complete this quest.
The Boss fight is divided in two phases, The Herald is first anchored to the ground, attacking the player and summoning Overminds.
Once 50% of it's health bar is depleted, the boss releases itself and flies over the battlefield.
Family:
Aetherial
Genus:
Aetherial Obelisk
Stage 1 (Anchored Form)
Abilities
Lightning Strike
Electrical Aura
Vindictive Aether
Summon Overmind
Skyshard Devastation
Lightning Discharge
Resistances
Level 3
Hero/Boss Resistances
Cold Resistance 15%
Fire Resistance 15%
Lightning Resistance 75%
Stage 2 (Flying Form)
Abilities
Lightning Strike
Electrical Aura
Vindictive Aether
Skyshard Devastation
Lightning Claw Spin
Earth Slam
Resistances
Level 3
Hero/Boss Resistances
Cold Resistance 15%
Fire Resistance 15%
Lightning Resistance 75%
Notable Drops
Supreme Spoils
Masteries

======================================================================
PAGE: Herald of the Flame
CATEGORIES: Aetherials, Boss Creatures
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Herald_of_the_Flame
======================================================================

The
Herald of the Flame
is a unique
Boss
creature that can be found in
The Conflagration
. It's located near the cannons required for the
Lost Armaments
quest.
The Boss fight is divided into two phases. The Herald is first anchored to the ground, attacking the player and summoning Fleshwarped Mutants.
Once 50% of its health bar is depleted, the boss releases itself and flies over the battlefield. He will then summon
Flesh Hulk
creatures.
Family:
Aetherial
Genus:
Aetherial Obelisk
Stage 1 (Anchored Form)
Abilities
Zap
Aether Strike
Despair Aura
Vindictive Aether
Summon
Fleshwarped Thrasher
Summon
Fleshwarped Butcher
Aether Conflagration
Resistances
Level 3
Hero/Boss Resistances
Cold Resistance 15%
Fire Resistance 75%
Lightning Resistance 15%
Stage 2 (Flying Form)
Abilities
Zap
Aether Strike
Despair Aura
Vindictive Aether
Summon
Flesh Hulk
Flame Wave
Aether Slam
Claw Spin
Aether Conflagration
Resistances
Level 3
Hero/Boss Resistances
Cold Resistance 15%
Fire Resistance 75%
Lightning Resistance 15%
Notable Drops
Supreme Spoils
Tip
As with seemingly all crystal formations, the first Phase can be outranged with a ranged weapon.

======================================================================
PAGE: Homestead (Faction)
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Homestead_(Faction)
======================================================================

Whenever trouble would strike the northern farmlands, the farmers and their families would rally at Homestead. This sense of community has proven more important than ever as humanity faces extinction.
With assistance from the Black Legion, Homestead was turned into a veritable fortress, a place of relative safety in a world ravaged by death; but with powerful enemies encroaching from every direction, some are beginning to wonder if entrenching in a fixed location was a wise decision.
While the Black Legion obsesses over the Aetherials and the Chthonians, the people of Homestead are focused on a threat much closer to home: the voracious Dermapteran swarm, which has spread rampantly and now infests their abandoned fields.
Homestead is an allied
Faction
within Grim Dawn. The player first encounters them in
Act 3
at
Homestead
.
Headquarters:  Homestead
Reputation gained from:
killing
Beasts
Quests
Bounties

======================================================================
PAGE: Kymon's Chosen
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Kymon's_Chosen
======================================================================

Kymon's Chosen are the zealous followers of Empyrion, the Light-Bringer, whom they believe will return to Cairn in blazing glory and guide his Chosen into a new dawn of order and prosperity.
Led by a charismatic preacher known as Father Kymon and guarded by their faith, the Chosen are eager to stamp out all corruption from Cairn, whether it is vile Chthonic horrors or death-manipulating necromancers. To this end, they consider ridding the world of the Order of Death's Vigil to be one of their utmost priorities.
Kymon's Chosen are one of the
Factions
in Grim Dawn. The player has the opportunity to side with this faction during the
Allies Among the Ashes
quest. Doing so enables access to Kymon's Chosen quests and bounties as well as the ability to purchase faction equipment. The player cannot choose this faction if they are already a
Necromancer
.  However, if the choice of Necromancer is delayed until after you join Kymon's they'll grumble, but won't throw you out.
Headquarters:
Kymon's Sanctuary
Reputation gained from:
Killing
Chthonians
Killing
Order of Death's Vigil
Quests
Bounties
Lore
Kymon's Chosen are followers of the enigmatic self-proclaimed prophet Thaidrus Kymon. Born to a great house of Erulan, Kymon grew up in the capital, witness to its many intrigues and scandals. Even as he was groomed by the members of his household to be the next great Kymon, Thaidrus was becoming disillusioned with the petty political rivalries and corruption around him. Much to the disappointment of his father, Thaidrus increasingly abandoned his political and martial training to immerse himself in books, devouring any writing on the pre-imperial histories and mythos that he could get his hands upon. He became fixated on myths from the dawn of humankind, when Empyrion the light-bringer was fabled to have ruled over the other gods, enforcing order in the world. Thaidrus became convinced the old myths were real and that only the return of Empyrion to the world could uplift humankind from their base existence. When his father took the extreme step of having the Kymon's vast library removed from the manor house and set ablaze, Thaidrus resolved to leave home and experience the vast world for himself.
It was on this journey that the noble came face to face with the poverty and misery that presided over much of the region around the capital. On more than one occasion, Thaidrus sadly discovered that the martial training of his formative years was of more value on the open road than the knowledge and lore he had amassed while engrossed in books. The journey opened Thaidrus' eyes to the harsh reality of the world, but it also wizened him to deeper truths. If people would not readily embrace change and the ideals he extolled, perhaps change had to be forced upon humankind for their own betterment.
Thaidrus returned to Erulan a changed man. He took up the mantle of his house once more and agreed to serve the empire, although not as a member of the court. He instead dedicated himself to the Luminari and eventually became an inquisitor. Outwardly, he seemed exemplary in his new role, zealous even but for Thaidrus it was a means to an end. As an inquisitor, Thaidrus had access to forbidden texts and knowledge thought too dangerous for the public that was kept locked away in the vaults of the Luminari. He encountered dark powers and accumulated esoteric lore traveling the far reaches of the empire. He collected many exotic artifacts, some of which he neglected to turn over to the Luminari.
One of his inquests led him to a remote village, built atop the ruins of an ancient city, which villagers claimed was the resting place of a forgotten god. The faded visage of the god, etched into the wall of a chamber below the city was similar to ancient stones he had seen in other parts of the empire. Although it was frightful to behold, Thaidrus thought perhaps it represented Empyrion and, if so, the ancient city might be the key to awakening him.
He spent many months there digging in the ruins, deciphering the dead language etched into crumbling blocks. Thaidrus repeatedly delayed reassignment by the citadel of the Luminari and finally ignored summons demanding his return. In time, Thaidrus mastered the dead language and pieced together the secrets of their religion. With trembling hands he prepared the grisly components needed to undertake the archaic ritual he had uncovered and, in a quavering voice, recited a passage in the dead language that would invoke the forgotten god. A pressure seemed to build in the air as a presence gathered in the dark chamber. Thaidrus felt it coiling around him and the hair on the back of his neck stood on end; he wondered if he had made a terrible mistake. A great intelligence invaded his mind and, in a burst of searing pain, it thrust a multitude of thoughts and feelings into his consciousness. It was almost too much to bear and it took several moments for Thaidrus to comprehend what the alien entity wanted and when he did, he recoiled. It offered great power but at a terrible price. Thaidrus summoned all of his mental discipline to push out the invader and used the arts of an inquisitor to sever the connection and ward himself from the entity.
Thaidrus left the village in flames; its inhabitants cast out into the wilderness. He returned to the capital, tired and defeated; ready to answer for his transgressions. However, he found that the head of his order was strangely indifferent to his absence. Thaidrus encountered other former acquaintances that seemed somehow changed and he noticed a pattern of peculiar behavior and events throughout the capital. It went unnoticed by most, but years of research and his experiences as an inquisitor had made Thaidrus especially observant of patterns in the world around him. Military regiments were removed from strategic locations and reassigned to defend positions of seemingly little importance, iron mines were shuttered, magistrates began to imprison citizens for minor offenses, and so forth. Thaidrus was disturbed and he quietly began his own inquisition of the empire itself.
His investigation came to a head when Thaidrus undertook the extreme measure of abducting the head of his order and putting him to the question. It was a perilous episode as his former superior eventually revealed himself to be possessed when he transformed and attacked Thaidrus. It was only with the power of an artifact taken from the chamber of the forgotten god that Thaidrus prevailed. He retreated to the manor house of the Kymons and considered his next move. The empire was in the grip of some malevolent otherworldly force, poised on the edge of oblivion, just waiting for the final stage of their great plan to unfold. It was the very thing that Thaidrus needed; the missing piece of his own plan that he had carried in his heart from childhood. It was the catalyst that would allow him to rebuild the world.
Now that Thaidrus saw the destruction of the empire and perhaps humankind itself was imminent, he knew his time had come. Perhaps it had been destined. He just needed the strength to survive the coming apocalypse and the power to claim his place as an architect of the new dawn. He knew, with apprehension, that the forgotten god could offer him all that.
In the days that followed, a great and unquenchable fire enveloped the Kymon manor house, killing all inside; all but one. Kymon the Prophet emerged to a crowd of spectators, untouched by the flames and he preached to the crowd of the coming apocalypse and of the salvation that he offered. Thaidrus was exiled from the capital soon thereafter but a devoted following left with him. These would become the first of Kymon's Chosen.
When the Grim Dawn struck, Kymon's Chosen were prepared to face the threat. Armed with the divine flame and Kymon's holy word, the Chosen set out to eradicate corruption and prepare the world for their ascension as the chosen people. Still, some cannot help but wonder if the promises Kymon makes are too good to be true. None but the Prophet himself has spoken to Empyrion, and none are allowed to observe when he communes with his god. Despite any doubts, the divine power Kymon wields cannot be denied and his mission of salvation brings hope in humanity's darkest hour.
Chief amongst his opponents are the followers of the Order of Death's Vigil, whom the Chosen see as abhorrent devil-spawn that twist the divine threads of life to their will. The extermination of the Order is chief among the Chosen's goals for Cairn's new dawn, something the necromancers naturally do not take kindly to. To the Order, Kymon is a fool and his followers even greater fools; but even a fool can be dangerous when armed. The Order will not allow history to repeat itself.

======================================================================
PAGE: Lagoth'Ak, Harbinger of Blood
CATEGORIES: Boss Creatures, Chthonics
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Lagoth'Ak,_Harbinger_of_Blood
======================================================================

Lagoth'Ak, Harbinger of Blood
is a unique
Boss
creature located in the third level of the
Bastion of Chaos
:
Anarchy
.
He is inside a
Death Room
, so the player must kill him in order to exit the area.
Family:
Chthonic
Genus:
Chthonian Harbinger
Abilities
Summon Void Leech
Summon Void Souldrinker
Mirage
Crimson Spout
Blood Orb Fan
Blood Orb Nova
Resistances
Level 4
Hero/Boss Resistances
Physical: 30%
Pierce: 25%
Fire: 15%
Cold: 15%
Lightning: 15%
Chaos: 100%
Knockdown: 500%
Petrify: 500%
Sleep:500%
Notable Drops
Magnificent Spoils
Blood of Ch'thon
Masteries

======================================================================
PAGE: Loghorrean, the Voice of Ch'thon
CATEGORIES: Boss Creatures, Chthonics
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Loghorrean,_the_Voice_of_Ch'thon
======================================================================

The
Loghorrean, the Voice of Ch'thon
is a unique
Boss
creature located in the
Edge of Madness
.
He is the final boss for
Act 4
and must be killed to access the next difficulty and complete
The Bane of Cairn
quest.
The fight is divided into 3 phases:
with
Ulgrim
fighting at the player's side.
when Loghorrean is at 65% health, Ulgrim is consumed by the void and two
Loghorrean's Vanguard
(Hero monsters) are summoned.
at 50% Health Loghorrean gains a 10% cast and attack speed increase and starts casting
Summon Void
.
Family:
Chthonic
Genus:
Voice of Ch'Thon
Quotes
"You say this thing has been summoned before?" - Player
"Yes, many centuries ago, at the Battle of Ikon. Nobody knows for sure whether it was the Carineans performing a last desperate maneuver, or perhaps the Loghorrean was drawn to all the spilled blood, but the results were catastrophic." -
Ulgrim

PAGE: Lunal'Valgoth, Steward of Darkness

This article is a
stub
. You can help Grim Dawn Wiki by
expanding it
.
This article is a
stub
. You can help Grim Dawn Wiki by
expanding it
.
Lunal'Valgoth, Steward of Darkness
is a
Chthonic
Insectoid
Boss located in the
Fringes of Sanity
. Lunal'Valgoth is also the target of the quest
Bounty: Steward of Darkness
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Malmouth Resistance
CATEGORIES: Factions
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Malmouth_Resistance
======================================================================

Once the pride of the east, Malmouth was an industrial marvel in the Erulan empire, with a bustling naval port and a thriving economy. It was also one of the first cities to fall during the events that unfolded on the day of the Grim Dawn.
Shattered from within by manipulative Aetherials, within mere weeks Malmouth turned from a proud beacon of the empire into a living nightmare for its denizens. But not all citizens of Malmouth perished when the fighting broke out. Some survived... endured... resisted.
These people now form the backbone of a resistance fighting to take back their city from the scheming invaders; but their resources are running low and human lives are finite when compared to the nigh infinite armies of the Aetherials that have entrenched themselves within Malmouth.
The
Malmouth Resistance
is one of the allied
Factions
in Grim Dawn. Members of this faction are first met in
Act 6
when playing the
Ashes of Malmouth
Expansion.
Headquarters:
Malmouth
Reputation gained from:
Killing
Aetherial Vanguard
Quests
Bounties
Quests
Bounties
Imps
Architects of Flesh
Regurgitators
Ascended
(Spellbreakers)
Blazebarf
Stormtitan
Blazragarus
Torraxsteria
Sanctuary
Aetherial Missive
Keeper of the Gates
Fleshshaper Hinissius

is a

Boss that is found in the

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Noveria Stormfire
CATEGORIES: Aetherials, Boss Creatures
URL: https://grimdawn.fandom.com/wiki/Noveria_Stormfire
======================================================================

Noveria Stormfire
is a unique
Boss
creature that can be found in
Stonerend Quarry
. Killing her is necessary to obtain the
Aetherial Essence
required to complete the
A Catalyst
quest.
Noveria will be encountered again as a Ghost during the
Service Beyond Death
quest.
Family:
Aetherial
-
Human
Genus:
Possessed
Abilities
Arcane Missile
Arcane Nova
Arcane Shield
Teleport
Aether Trap
Resistances
Level 4
Hero/Boss Resistances
Aether Resistance 30%
Lightning Resistance 30%
Notable Drops
Grand Spoils
Aetherial Essence
(Quest)
Masteries
